Add-on Treatment.evidence: Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.
Add-on Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is Darolutamide. This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 1: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." This indicates that darolutamide (or placebo) is added to the standard backbone of ADT and docetaxel. The "Trial Design" section on page 2 further confirms this: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel."

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.plan_extraction_plan: From Table 3, under "All Patients" and "Darolutamide (n = 652)", sum the counts and percentages for "Grade 3", "Grade 4", and "Grade 5 (death)". Grade 3: 248 (38.0%), Grade 4: 183 (28.1%), Grade 5 (death): 27 (4.1%). Total: 248 + 183 + 27 = 458 patients. Total percentage: 38.0% + 28.1% + 4.1% = 70.2%. Value: 458 (70.2%)

From Table 3, under "All Patients" and "Placebo (n = 650)", sum the counts and percentages for "Grade 3", "Grade 4", and "Grade 5 (death)". Grade 3: 232 (35.7%), Grade 4: 181 (27.8%), Grade 5 (death): 26 (4.0%). Total: 232 + 181 + 26 = 439 patients. Total percentage: 35.7% + 27.8% + 4.0% = 67.5%. Value: 439 (67.5%)

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Table 3 reports "Treatment-Emergent AEs" by grade, which are generally considered all-cause. It does not specify which of these AEs are "treatment-related" for Grade 3 or higher. The text also does not provide this specific breakdown.

Table 3 reports "Treatment-Emergent AEs" by grade, which are generally considered all-cause. It does not specify which of these AEs are "treatment-related" for Grade 3 or higher. The text also does not provide this specific breakdown.

Table 3 reports "Grade 5 (death)" under "Worst grade" for "Any AE", which is generally considered all-cause treatment-emergent. It does not specify if these Grade 5 events are "treatment-related". Footnote 'd' for Table 3 states "Specific grade 5 events are provided in the supplementary appendix of Smith et al.", implying these are all-cause.

Table 3 reports "Grade 5 (death)" under "Worst grade" for "Any AE", which is generally considered all-cause treatment-emergent. It does not specify if these Grade 5 events are "treatment-related". Footnote 'd' for Table 3 states "Specific grade 5 events are provided in the supplementary appendix of Smith et al.", implying these are all-cause.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.plan_extraction_plan: This column refers to the overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence in randomized controlled trials. This type of assessment (e.g., risk of bias assessment) is typically performed by external reviewers in systematic reviews or meta-analyses, not usually reported within a primary clinical trial publication itself. The ARASENS trial report does not contain an overall risk judgment of its own individual criteria of evidence.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: androgen receptor pathway inhibitor, hormonal therapy, chemotherapy
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide orplacebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.chunk_id: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1::1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.plan_extraction_plan: The class of agents in Treatment Arm 1 (Darolutamide + ADT + Docetaxel) is reported in the abstract and introduction. Darolutamide is an androgen receptor pathway inhibitor, ADT is androgen-deprivation therapy (a type of hormonal therapy), and docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent.

Control Arm.value: placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
Control Arm.evidence: Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide orplacebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.
Control Arm.plan_extraction_plan: The control arm is explicitly stated in the abstract and methods section. It can also be inferred from the labels in figures and tables. On page 1, in the "METHODS" section of the abstract, it states: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." On page 2, in the "Trial Design" section under "METHODS", it states: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." In Figure 1 (CONSORT flow diagram) on page 3, one of the main arms is labeled "Placebo + docetaxel + ADT." In Figure 2 (OS in subgroups) on page 6, the legend for the control arm is "Placebo + ADT + docetaxel." In Figure 3 (Time to castration-resistant prostate cancer) on page 7, the legend for the control arm is "Placebo + ADT + docetaxel."

Control Arm - N.evidence:
As previously reported, a total of 1,306 patients were randomly assigned, and 1,305 patients were included in the full analysis set of ARASENS (Fig 1). Of them, 1,005
(77%) patients had high-volume disease and 300 (23%) had low-volume disease; 912 (70%) patients had high-risk
disease, and 393 (30%) had low-risk disease (Table 1).
Control Arm - N.plan_extraction_plan: On page 3, refer to the CONSORT flow diagram (Figure 1). Locate the box for "Placebo + docetaxel + ADT" and find the number of patients "Analyzed for primary end point", which is n = 654. Alternatively, on page 5, refer to Table 1, "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS". In the "All Patients (N = 1,305)" column, under the "Placebo" sub-column, the total number of patients is 654.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: Figure 1 (Chunk 0) on page 3 shows that 651 patients in the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT arm received allocated treatment.
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: The abstract on page 1 (Chunk 8) states, "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The Methods section on page 2 (Chunk 8) further clarifies, "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." The Discussion section on page 6 (Chunk 9) explicitly confirms, "The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel, show that darolutamide in combination with ADT and docetaxel increases OS in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Figure 1 (Chunk 0) on page 3 shows that 651 patients in the Darolutamide + docetaxel + ADT arm received allocated treatment. Therefore, 651 patients (100%) in the treatment arm received docetaxel.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: Figure 1 (Chunk 0) on page 3 shows that 651 patients in the Placebo + docetaxel + ADT arm received allocated treatment.
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: The abstract on page 1 (Chunk 8) states, "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." The Methods section on page 2 (Chunk 8) further clarifies, "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." The Discussion section on page 6 (Chunk 9) explicitly confirms, "The results of this post hoc analysis of the ARASENS trial, in which all patients received docetaxel, show that darolutamide in combination with ADT and docetaxel increases OS in patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." Figure 1 (Chunk 0) on page 3 shows that 651 patients in the Placebo + docetaxel + ADT arm received allocated treatment. Therefore, 651 patients (100%) in the control arm received docetaxel.

Number of Arms Included.plan_extraction_plan: The trial design section on page 2 states, "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel." This indicates two arms: one receiving darolutamide plus ADT and docetaxel, and the other receiving placebo plus ADT and docetaxel. OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group).
OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: Page: 3. Source: Figure 1, text below the diagram. "The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group)." The total number of patients analyzed for the primary endpoint in the darolutamide group was 651 (from Figure 1, "Analyzed for primary end point"). Value: Calculate as (Total patients - Number of deaths) / Total patients * 100 = (651 - 229) / 651 * 100 = 64.82%.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group).
OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.plan_extraction_plan: Page: 3. Source: Figure 1, text below the diagram. "The primary end point (OS) was assessed after 533 patients had died (229 in the darolutamide group and 304 in the placebo group)." The total number of patients analyzed for the primary endpoint in the placebo group was 654 (from Figure 1, "Analyzed for primary end point"). Value: Calculate as (Total patients - Number of deaths) / Total patients * 100 = (654 - 304) / 654 * 100 = 53.52%.

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: Page: 16. Source: Figure A1, Panel A, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 181/432). This represents the high-volume synchronous subgroup. Page: 17. Source: Figure A1, Panel B, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Darolutamide column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 25/126). This represents the low-volume synchronous subgroup. Value: Combine data from both subgroups. Total patients = 432 + 126 = 558. Total events = 181 + 25 = 206. Calculate as (Total patients - Total events) / Total patients * 100 = (558 - 206) / 558 * 100 = 63.08%.

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Control.plan_extraction_plan: Page: 16. Source: Figure A1, Panel A, row "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis - De novo", Placebo column ("No. of Events/No. of Patients": 239/445). This represents the high-volume synchronous subgroup. Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: OS was the primary end point.

Quality of Life Scale.plan_extraction_plan: The PDF reports on "time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire" (page 3, text, "Study Assessments" section, and page 8, Table 2). While this is a patient-reported outcome related to quality of life, it specifically measures physical symptoms rather than an overall "Quality of Life Scale" assessing general well-being and satisfaction with health status as per the definition. Therefore, an overall Quality of Life Scale is not reported.

Quality of Life reported.plan_extraction_plan: Yes. The clinical trial reports results on quality of life. On page 3, in the "Study Assessments" section, it states: "Secondary efficacy end points included ... time to worsening of disease-related physical symptoms as measured by the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate (National Comprehensive Cancer Network) symptom index 17-item questionnaire". Table 2 on page 8 also lists "Time to worsening disease-related symptoms" as a secondary efficacy end point.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.plan_extraction_plan: Yes. The text on page 2, in the "METHODS" section, states: "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." "De novo" refers to synchronous disease. Additionally, Table 1 on page 5, under "Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)", includes "De novo mHSPC" as a subgroup. Figure A1 on page 16 also presents OS results for the "De novo" subgroup under "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis".

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.plan_extraction_plan: Yes. The text on page 2, in the "METHODS" section, states: "In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer." "Recurrent" refers to metachronous disease. Additionally, Table 1 on page 5, under "Metastasis stage at initial diagnosis, No. (%)", includes "Recurrent mHSPC" as a subgroup. Figure A1 on page 16 also presents OS results for the "Recurrent" subgroup under "Metastatic stage at initial diagnosis".

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: We examined efﬁcacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.plan_extraction_plan: Yes. The "PURPOSE" and "RESULTS" sections on page 1 explicitly mention reporting by disease volume. The "METHODS" section on page 2 defines "High-volume disease". Table 1 on page 5 is titled "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS" and includes a "High Volume" column. Figure 2A on page 6 shows OS for "high-volume disease". Table 2 on page 8, Table 3 on page 9, and Table 4 on page 10 all present data for the "High Volume" subgroup. Figure A1 on page 16 also presents OS results for the "High Volume" subgroup.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: We examined efﬁcacy and safety from the ARASENS trial for subgroups by disease volume and risk.
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.plan_extraction_plan: Yes. The "PURPOSE" and "RESULTS" sections on page 1 explicitly mention reporting by disease volume. The "METHODS" section on page 2 defines "Low-volume and low-risk disease". Table 1 on page 5 is titled "Baseline Demographics and Patient Characteristics for the Subgroups of Patients by Disease Volume and Disease Risk in ARASENS" and includes a "Low Volume" column. Figure 2B on page 6 shows OS for "low-volume disease". Table 2 on page 8, Table 3 on page 9, and Table 4 on page 10 all present data for the "Low Volume" subgroup. Figure A1 on page 17 also presents OS results for the "Low Volume" subgroup.
